# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

workflow_dispatch:
  inputs:
    pr_title:
      type: string
      description: "PR Title"
      required: true
    pr_description:
      type: string
      description: "PR Description"
      required: true

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]

jobs:
  build_and_notify:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set PR Title and Description for workflow_dispatch
        id: set_pr_details
        run: |
          echo "pr_title=${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "pr_description=${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'

      - name: Use PR Title and Description from workflow_dispatch or event
        run: |
          PR_TITLE="${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.pr_title || steps.set_pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}"
          PR_DESCRIPTION="${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.pr_description || steps.set_pr_details.outputs.pr_description }}"
          
          echo "PR Title: $PR_TITLE"
          echo "PR Description: $PR_DESCRIPTION"

          # Example: Using PR_TITLE in a commit message (if you were to commit something)
          # git config user.name github-actions[bot]
          # git config user.email github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com
          # git commit -m "Build triggered for: $PR_TITLE" --allow-empty # Example commit

          # Example: Sending a notification (replace with your actual notification system)
          # For Slack, you might use a curl command or a dedicated action
          # For email, you might use an email action
          echo "Sending notification about build for PR: \"$PR_TITLE\""
          echo "Description: $PR_DESCRIPTION"
          
          # Placeholder for actual build steps
          echo "Running build steps here..."
          # For example: npm install, npm test, docker build, etc.
          
          echo "Build completed successfully for PR: \"$PR_TITLE\""

      # Example: Post build notification with PR details to a custom service
      # - name: Send custom build notification
      #   env:
      #     PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.pr_title || steps.set_pr_details.outputs.pr_title }}
      #     PR_DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' && github.event.inputs.pr_description || steps.set_pr_details.outputs.pr_description }}
      #     WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL }} # Store your webhook URL in GitHub Secrets
      #   run: |
      #     if [ -n "$WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
      #       curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{
      #         \"text\": \"🚀 Build triggered for PR: *$PR_TITLE*\",
      #         \"attachments\": [
      #           {
      #             \"text\": \"Description: $PR_DESCRIPTION\\nLink: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url || github.event.workflow_run.pull_requests[0].html_url }}\",
      #             \"color\": \"#36a64f\"
      #           }
      #         ]
      #       }" "$WEBHOOK_URL"
      #     else
      #       echo "WEBHOOK_URL not set, skipping custom notification."
      #     fi
